headline: CERTIFY ONLY WHAT THE DOSSIER PERMITS
subheading: Keep every QP batch release in scope, defensible and audit-ready under MHRA inspection.
flux_prompt: An abstract editorial conceptual composition evoking pharmaceutical batch certification staying within the legal scope of a marketing authorisation. Centre on a luminous translucent dossier of stacked regulatory documents glowing teal at the edges, perfectly aligned and overlapping with the silhouette of a single finished medicinal batch and a floating row of vials, the two forms registering exactly within a precise framed boundary to symbolise conformity and staying in scope. A delicate cyan double-helix ribbon threads through the layers connecting dossier to batch, while a faint outlined boundary frame and a subtle approval seal motif suggest authorisation without any text. One pending element drifts slightly outside the frame, greyed and held back, contrasting the approved against the unapproved variation. Deep navy background with layered depth, polished reflective surface beneath, volumetric teal and cyan light beams, a single fresh-green indicator glow as a focal accent, fine particle detail and shallow depth of field, clean and premium clinical-luxury aesthetic, precise authoritative atmosphere. 1500x900 composition, 5:3 aspect ratio, professional editorial illustration, premium quality, clean composition, modern visual storytelling, professional lighting, no text, no watermark, no logo, ultra detailed
